title: Lucas G. da Silva
text:
Sou graduando em Ciências Contábeis pela Universidade Federal da Paraíba (UFPB). Fui intercambista pelo programa Gira Mundo (2018/2019) em Ontário, Canadá. Consegui uma bolsa de estudos para a escola de Idiomas CCAA, onde conclui o curso de língua inglesa em 2020. Fui professor de inglês em 2021.
Hoje, além da minha graduação, estudo Data Science e ferramentas adjacentes, como R/Tidyverse, PowerBI, SQL e Python.

title: Finance charts
text:
Interactive charts such as OHLC charts, candlesticks, and time series are quite useful for data presentation, you can effortlessly create them using libraries like plotly, ggplotly, ggplot, and highcharter.
The simplicity of these R packages bring to visualizing market trends, making financial analyses more insightful and engaging.

title: Finance charts
Finance charts
text:
Plotly OHLC chart

# This time, get tidy Apple stock data with tidyquant

apple_stocks <- tq_get(
    "AAPL",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2010-01-01",
    to = "2023-12-31"
)

# Plot basic interactive OHLC chart with plotly

apple_stocks |> 
    plot_ly(
        x =     ~date,
        type =  "ohlc",
        open =  ~open,
        close = ~close,
        high =  ~high,
        low =   ~low
    )

title: Finance charts
text:
Plotly candlestick plot

# Get tidy data of NVIDIA stocks with tidyquant

nvidia_stocks <- tq_get(
    "NVDA",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2010-01-01",
    to = "2023-12-31"
)

# Plot basic interactive candlestick chart with plotly

nvidia_stocks |> 
    plot_ly(
        x = ~date,
        type = "candlestick",
        open = ~open,
        close = ~close,
        high = ~high,
        low = ~low
    ) |> 
    layout(
        title = "Basic candlestick chart",
        xaxis = list(rangeslider = list(visible = FALSE))
    )

title: Finance charts
section: Basic time series chart with ggplot and plotly
text:
Basic time series chart with ggplot and plotly

# Get amazon stock data again, but this time tidy data
# with tidyquant

amazon_stocks <- tq_get(
    "AMZN",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2000-01-01",
    to = "2016-12-31"
)

# Tell ggplot how to build the chart

amzn_time_series <- amazon_stocks |> 
    ggplot(aes(date, adjusted)) +
    geom_line(color = palette_light()[[1]]) + 
    labs(
        title = "AMZN Line Chart", 
        subtitle = "Continuous Scale", 
        y = "Closing Price",
        x = element_blank()
    ) + 
    theme_tq()

# Call plotly with ggplotly

ggplotly(amzn_time_series)

title: Interactive Finance Charts
text:
Interactive charts such as OHLC charts, candlesticks, and time series are quite useful for data presentation. You can effortlessly create them using libraries like plotly, ggplotly, ggplot, and highcharter.
The simplicity of these R packages bring to visualizing market trends, making financial analyses more insightful and engaging.


Code
# Install and load required packages

packages <- c(
    "tidyverse", "highcharter", "quantmod",
    "plotly", "tidyquant"
)

installed_packages <- packages %in% row.names(installed.packages())

if (any(installed_packages == FALSE)) {
    
    install.packages(
        packages[!installed_packages],
        dependencies = TRUE
    )

}

invisible(lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))

title: Interactive Finance Charts
Interactive Finance Charts
text:
Plotly OHLC chart
A simple OHCL chart with plotly and tidyquant.


Code
# This time, get tidy Apple stocks data with tidyquant

apple_stocks <- tq_get(
    "AAPL",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2010-01-01",
    to = "2023-12-31"
)

# Plot basic interactive OHLC chart with plotly

apple_stocks |> 
    plot_ly(
        x =     ~date,
        type =  "ohlc",
        open =  ~open,
        close = ~close,
        high =  ~high,
        low =   ~low
    ) |> 
    layout(
        title = "Basic OHLC chart",
        xaxis = list(rangeslider = list(visible = FALSE)),
        hovermode = "x"
    )

title: Interactive Finance Charts
text:
Plotly candlestick plot
Candlestick charts are also quickly and easly done with plotly and tidyquant.


Code
# Get tidy data of NVIDIA stocks with tidyquant

nvidia_stocks <- tq_get(
    "NVDA",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2010-01-01",
    to = "2023-12-31"
)

# Plot basic interactive candlestick chart with plotly

nvidia_stocks |> 
    plot_ly(
        x =     ~date,
        type =  "candlestick",
        open =  ~open,
        close = ~close,
        high =  ~high,
        low =   ~low
    ) |> 
    layout(
        title = "Basic candlestick chart",
        xaxis = list(rangeslider = list(visible = FALSE)),
        hovermode = "x"
    )

title: Interactive Finance Charts
section: Basic time series chart with ggplot and plotly
text:
Basic time series chart with ggplot and plotly
Combine plotly and ggplot2 with ggplotly to get easy, tidy, and interactive financial charts.


Code
# Get amazon stock data again, but this time tidy data
# with tidyquant

amazon_stocks <- tq_get(
    "AMZN",
    get = "stock.prices",
    from = "2000-01-01",
    to = "2016-12-31"
)

# Tell ggplot how to build the chart

amzn_time_series <- amazon_stocks |> 
    ggplot(aes(date, adjusted)) +
    geom_line(color = palette_light()[[1]]) + 
    labs(
        title =    "AMZN Line Chart", 
        subtitle = "Continuous Scale", 
        y =        "Closing Price",
        x =        element_blank()
    ) + 
    theme_tq()

# Call plotly with ggplotly

ggplotly(amzn_time_series)
